channel.title: Integration on Eric Windsor
channel.description: Recent content in Integration on Eric Windsor

item.title: Multiple integration
item.description: Iterated Integrals and Area Iterated integration is the process of repeatedly integrating the results of previous integrations. Integrating one integral is denoted as follows. Let $a, b, c$ and $d$ be numbers and let $g_1 (x)$, $g_2 (x)$, $h_1 (y)$ and $h_2 (y)$ be functions of $x$ and $y$, respectively. Then: $$ \begin{align} \int_c^d \int_{h_1(y)}^{h_2(y)}f(x,y)dx dy = \int_c^d(\int_{h_1(y)}^{h_2(y)}f(x,y)dx)dy\\\\ \int_a^b \int_{g_1(y)}^{g_2(y)}f(x,y)dx dy = \int_a^b (\int_{g_1(y)}^{g_2(y)}f(x,y)dy) dx \end{align} $$ $x$ varies from $h_1(y)$ to $h_2(y)$, whereas $y$ varies from $c$ to $d$.
